data.title: Shiba Inu Sets Up Never Before Seen Signal on Weekly Chart: Details
data.description:
Shiba Inu is eyeing a never-before-seen setup on its weekly chart as the weekly moving averages (MA) 50 and 200 draw closer.

This is significant as the drawing closer of the moving averages with an imminent convergence might highlight a potential death cross or, on the contrary, a golden cross.

This would be the first time on record such a technical pattern would appear on Shiba Inu's weekly chart.

A death cross would occur in the coming weeks if Shiba Inu's 50-week MA crosses under its 200-week SMA, and a golden cross would appear in the reverse scenario. Weekly Chart, Courtesy: TradingView">

A death cross on the weekly time frame makes for a cautious view of the short-term outlook and maintains the potential for a return. One could argue that the death cross timing might be right for bears as the market faces increased uncertainty.

A moving average-based death cross represents what happened in the past, with some still considering it a forward-looking indicator.

Some analysts, however, do not see the death cross as a reliable indicator because it is based on backward-looking moving averages and lags prices and has proved to be a contrary indicator in some cases.

A death cross on Shiba Inu's weekly chart might turn out to be a nonevent, indicating that the token is oversold after months of lackluster trading action.

SHIB declines amid market drop

Shiba Inu has entered its fourth day of dropping since Nov. 10, coinciding with a broader market sell-off.

A sharp liquidity crunch sent Bitcoin and altcoins plunging on Friday, triggering over $1.28 billion in liquidations, according to CoinGlass data.

Shiba Inu was not exempt, dropping to a low of $0.00000893 on Friday. At press time, SHIB was down 6.93% in the last 24 hours to $0.000009.

The altcoin market will be depending on whether Bitcoin can find its way out of danger and return above crucial support.

data.title: US drops Biden plan to require passenger compensation for delayed flights
data.description:
By David Shepardson

President Donald Trump's administration said on Friday it was formally withdrawing a plan by his predecessor to require airlines to pay passengers cash compensation when U.S. flight disruptions are caused by carriers.

In December 2024, the U.S. Transportation Department under then-President Joe Biden sought public comment on writing rules to require airlines to pay $200 to $300 for domestic delays of at least three hours and up to $775 for longer delays.

In September, USDOT first disclosed it planned to withdraw the proposal. On Friday, USDOT said the rules would result in "unnecessary regulatory burdens" in explaining why it would not go forward.

Last month, a group of 18 Democratic senators urged the Trump administration not to drop the compensation plan.

"This is a common-sense proposal: when an airline’s mistake imposes unanticipated costs on families, the airline should try to remedy the situation by providing accommodations to consumers and helping cover their costs," said the letter signed by Democratic Senators Richard Blumenthal, Maria Cantwell, Ed Markey and others.

Airlines in the U.S. must refund passengers for canceled flights, but are not required to compensate customers for delays. The European Union, Canada, Brazil and Britain all have airline delay compensation rules. No large U.S. airline currently guarantees cash compensation for significant flight disruption.

USDOT said Friday in abandoning the compensation plan that it would "allow airlines to compete on the services and compensation that they provide to passengers rather than imposing new minimum requirements for these services and compensation through regulation, which would impose significant costs on airlines."

USDOT also announced in September it was considering rescinding Biden regulations requiring airlines and ticket agents to disclose service fees alongside airfares.

It also plans to reduce regulatory burdens on airlines and ticket agents by writing new rules detailing the definition of a flight cancellation that entitles consumers to ticket refunds, as well as revisiting rules on ticket pricing and advertising.

Airlines have pushed the Trump administration to go further and praised the action to rescind the Biden plan.

data.title: Wall Street Continues Sharp Sell-Off
data.description:
US stocks moved lower on Friday, with the S&P 500 down 1% and the Nasdaq falling 1.6%, both hitting one-month lows, while the Dow Jones dropped about 400 points.

The tech sector remained under heavy pressure as investors grew increasingly concerned about stretched valuations in AI-related stocks and the risk of a bubble.

Concerns also mounted that the Fed may hesitate to cut rates next month, after several policymakers expressed skepticism about the need for another reduction.

Market odds of a 25 bps cut in December have fallen to just below 50%, down from nearly 65% earlier in the week.

Consumer discretionary and communication services shares were sharply lower.

Nvidia slid about 2.7%, Tesla dropped 3.4%, and Applied Materials fell more than 5.5% after reporting earnings and revenue misses.

Verizon, meanwhile, rose 0.5% amid reports the company is considering 20,000 job cuts.

For the week, the S&P 500 is up around 0.1%, the Dow has gained roughly 1%, and the Nasdaq is down 0.6%.

data.title: First Solar dips, to invest $330 mln to build South Carolina plant
data.description:
** Shares of U.S.-based solar panel maker First Solar fall 4.6% at $244.72

** Co to invest $330 mln to build South Carolina facility for Series 6 Plus module production

** The facility is forecast to create over 600 new jobs with an average manufacturing salary of $74,000 per year, about twice the per capita income in Cherokee County - First Solar

** "The passage of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act and the Administration's trade policies boosted demand for American energy technology, requiring a timely, agile response that allows us to meet the moment" - CEO Mark Widmar

** Co expects to commence commercial operations in the second half of 2026

** Including sessions move, shares up 45.6% YTD

data.title: INTERNATIONAL ISOTOPES INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
International Isotopes Inc., a company specializing in the production of nuclear medicine and industrial isotopes, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025. The company has faced a mix of challenges and growth opportunities across its various business segments.

Financial Highlights

Sale of Product: $3.28 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, a decrease of 16% compared to the same period in 2024 due to decreased sales in Theranostics Products.

Sale of Product: $10.17 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, an increase of 2% compared to the same period in 2024, driven by increased sales in Calibration & Reference Products.

Gross Profit: $1.94 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, a decrease of 21% compared to the same period in 2024, primarily due to decreased sales in Theranostics Products.

Gross Profit: $6.11 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, a decrease of 3% compared to the same period in 2024, impacted by decreased sales in Theranostics Products.

Net (loss) income: $(203,601) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to net income of $150,251 for the same period in 2024, largely due to decreased sales in Theranostics Products.

Net (loss) income: $(477,080) for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to a net loss of $(237,245) for the same period in 2024, due to decreased sales in Theranostics Products and Cobalt Products.

Net loss (income) per common share - basic: $— for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025, consistent with the same periods in 2024.

Business Highlights

Theranostics Products Performance: The Theranostics Products segment, which includes FDA-approved sodium iodide I-131 drug products, experienced a decrease in sales by 32% for the three months and 17% for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to the same periods in 2024. This was due to temporary supplier outages and partial loss of business from a national account. The company expects stabilized sales and growth from new theranostic API products.

Cobalt Products Performance: Sales in the Cobalt Products segment decreased by 6% for the three months and 8% for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to the same periods in 2024. The decrease was attributed to the timing of cobalt sealed source manufacturing sales. Despite this, the segment reported increased gross profit due to reduced cost of products.

Calibration & Reference Products Performance: This segment saw a 4% increase in sales for the three months and a significant 57% increase for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to the same periods in 2024. The increase was due to the resolution of a global shortage of Cobalt-57 isotopes that affected sales in 2024.

Medical Device Products Launch: The Medical Device Products segment, which started in 2024, reported sales of $65,757 for the three months and $176,476 for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. The segment is developing new products, including the Swirler® and Tru-Fit™ Mouthpiece, expected to launch in early 2026.

Fluorine Products Divestiture: The company is in the process of divesting its Fluorine Products segment, with an expected sale closure by March 2026. This divestiture is anticipated to bring in $12.5 million, which will be used to pay down related notes and dissolve the segment.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates stabilized sales and growth in the Theranostics Products segment following the resolution of supplier issues. Additionally, the divestiture of the Fluorine Products segment is expected to positively impact the company's financial position by reducing liabilities and increasing cash reserves.

SEC Filing: INTERNATIONAL ISOTOPES INC [ INIS ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Fluent, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Fluent, Inc., a leading data-driven performance marketing company, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational challenges faced by the company, including a notable decline in revenue and increased operational losses. Despite these challenges, the company has shown some improvement in net loss and loss per share metrics.

Financial Highlights

For the three months ended September 30, 2025, Fluent, Inc. reported the following key financial metrics:

  • Total Revenue: $47.03 million, a decrease from $64.52 million in the same period in 2024, indicating a decline in sales performance.
  • Loss from Operations: $(6.30) million, compared to $(3.89) million in the same period in 2024, indicating increased operational losses.
  • Net Loss: $(7.56) million, compared to $(7.94) million in the same period in 2024, showing a slight improvement in net loss.
  • Basic and Diluted Loss Per Share: $(0.27), compared to $(0.48) in the same period in 2024, reflecting an improvement in loss per share.

For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, the company reported:

  • Total Revenue: $146.95 million, compared to $189.22 million in the same period in 2024, showing a significant reduction in revenue over the period.
  • Loss from Operations: $(20.49) million, compared to $(18.22) million in the same period in 2024, reflecting a worsening operational performance.
  • Net Loss: $(23.05) million, compared to $(25.85) million in the same period in 2024, indicating a reduction in net loss.
  • Basic and Diluted Loss Per Share: $(0.94), compared to $(1.75) in the same period in 2024, showing a significant improvement in loss per share.

Business Highlights

Fluent, Inc.'s business performance for the third quarter of 2025 included the following key metrics and activities:

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported revenue from several segments:
    • Owned and Operated: $20,744
    • Commerce Media Solutions: $18,808
    • Call Solutions: $5,283
    • AdParlor: $2,183
  • Future Outlook: The company is facing challenges in achieving profitability due to declining revenue and difficulties in sourcing traffic for its digital media properties. Management is considering further cost reduction measures and resource reallocation to meet projected budgets and cash flow requirements. The company is also monitoring business unit performance for potential divestments.

SEC Filing: Fluent, Inc. [ FLNT ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: FRANKLIN WIRELESS CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Franklin Wireless Corp, a leading provider of integrated wireless solutions utilizing 5G and 4G LTE technologies, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, highlighting key metrics and business activities that have shaped the quarter.

Financial Highlights

Net Sales: $12.74 million, decreased by 4.3% compared to the same period in 2024 due to decreased demand from major carrier customers.

Gross Profit: $2.91 million, increased by 40.5% from the corresponding period in 2024, driven by an increased proportion of high-margin sales and decreased production costs.

Income (loss) from operations: $0.59 million, representing 4.6% of net sales, compared to a loss of $0.37 million in the same period in 2024.

Net Income: $0.55 million, decreased from $0.65 million in the corresponding period of 2024, primarily due to unfavorable changes in foreign currency and the absence of a one-time gain recognized in the prior-year period.

Net Income attributable to Parent Company: $0.64 million, increased from $0.52 million in the corresponding period of 2024.

Earnings per share attributable to Parent Company stockholders - basic: $0.05, compared to $0.04 in the same period in 2024.

Earnings per share attributable to Parent Company stockholders - diluted: $0.05, compared to $0.04 in the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

Geographical Performance: For the three months ended September 30, 2025, net sales in North America accounted for 99.9% of total net sales, while Asia contributed 0.1%. North America experienced a decrease in net sales by 4.4% compared to the same period in 2024, primarily due to decreased demand from major carrier customers. Conversely, sales in Asia increased significantly by 2,466.6%, although from a very low base.

Product and Service Offerings: Franklin Wireless Corp. continues to focus on integrated wireless solutions utilizing 5G and 4G LTE technologies, including mobile hotspots, fixed wireless routers, and mobile device management (MDM) solutions. The company is also involved in the Internet of Things (IoT) and machine-to-machine (M2M) applications.

Operational Efficiency: The company reported an increase in gross profit margin to 22.8% for the three months ended September 30, 2025, up from 15.5% in the same period of 2024. This improvement was attributed to a higher proportion of high-margin sales and decreased production costs.

Research and Development: Research and development expenses decreased by 7.3% for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to the same period in 2024. This was primarily due to a reduction in direct R&D costs, which fluctuate from period to period.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates that its revenue growth will be influenced by maintaining existing customer relationships, increasing demand for wireless data products, customer acceptance of new products, and the ability to meet customer demands. Additionally, the company is working to enhance its software service offerings to address changes in market demand, particularly as remote education and work-from-home trends decline post-pandemic.

SEC Filing: FRANKLIN WIRELESS CORP [ FKWL ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: ENB Financial Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
ENB Financial Corp, a prominent financial services provider operating in Lancaster County, southeastern Lebanon County, and southwestern Berks County, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational achievements, underscoring the company's robust performance in a competitive market.

Financial Highlights

Total Interest and Dividend Income: ENB Financial Corp reported a total interest and dividend income of $27.065 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025. This marks a notable increase from $23.506 million in the same period of 2024, driven by higher interest and fees on loans and securities.

Net Interest Income: The company's net interest income rose to $17.575 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, up from $14.170 million in the same period of 2024. This improvement reflects increased interest income despite a slight rise in interest expense.

Net Income: ENB Financial Corp achieved a net income of $5.919 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, representing a substantial 77.4% increase compared to $3.336 million for the same period in 2024. This growth is attributed to higher net interest income and controlled operating expenses.

Earnings Per Share of Common Stock: The earnings per share of common stock were $1.04 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $0.59 for the same period in 2024, reflecting a 76.3% increase and highlighting the strong growth in net income.

Operating Expenses: Operating expenses for the three months ended September 30, 2025, were $15.181 million, up from $14.124 million in the same period of 2024. The increase is primarily due to higher investments in technology and acquisition-related costs.

Provision for Credit Losses: The provision for credit losses was significantly reduced to $20,000 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $497,000 in the same period of 2024. This reduction reflects adjustments in economic qualitative factors and loan growth.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: ENB Financial Corp's primary revenue streams are derived from interest and dividends earned on loans, investment securities, and other financial instruments. The company has evaluated its contracts with customers and determined that further disaggregation of revenue into more granular categories beyond what is presented in the Consolidated Statements of Income was not necessary.

Geographical Performance: The company provides a variety of financial services to individuals and small businesses in Lancaster County, southeastern Lebanon County, and southwestern Berks County through its branch network. Its primary deposit products include checking, savings, and term certificate accounts, while its primary lending products encompass commercial, agricultural, residential and construction mortgages, small business, and consumer loans.

Sales Units: ENB Financial Corp's loan portfolio includes agriculture, business loans, consumer loans, home equity, non-owner occupied commercial real estate, and residential real estate. The total net loans as of September 30, 2025, were $1,465,638,000.

Future Outlook: The company has entered into a definitive agreement with Cecil Bancorp, Inc., which is expected to close in the first quarter of 2026. This acquisition will expand ENB Financial Corp's presence with 18 full-service community banking offices in Pennsylvania and Maryland, offering a full range of integrated financial services including banking and trust.

SEC Filing: ENB Financial Corp [ ENBP ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Bank of England weighs easing life insurer access to capital markets
data.description:
The Bank of England said on Friday it would consider changing rules to make it easier for life insurers to access capital markets, after industry players voiced concerns about it becoming harder to raise funds in equity and debt markets.

The Prudential Regulation Authority, which sits within the BoE, said it was examining possible tweaks to its Insurance Special Purpose Vehicle regime as well as other methods used in sectors such as banking.

An ISPV is a PRA-authorised entity used to transfer risk to capital markets.

Currently, ISPVs are not allowed to transfer life insurance business to capital markets, because they can be longer term and more complex than general insurance where legal structures are more straightforward and assets held are low risk.

However, the PRA said it was open to reforming those rules.

Britain's large life insurance sector has more than 2 trillion pounds ($2.7 trillion) of assets, according to the BoE. Aviva , Legal & General and Phoenix are among the biggest players.

The regulator is not proposing specific rule changes yet and is instead seeking input from stakeholders on their capital needs, what form that capital should take and structures that could improve access.

Its discussion paper has also asked for views on how regulators and firms should manage risks created by greater flexibility in accessing funding.

($1 = 0.7451 pounds)

data.title: Union Pacific, Norfolk shareholders approve $85 billion merger
data.description:
Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern shareholders approved a deal to merge both railroad operators, the companies said on Friday.

Over 99% of shareholders at both Union Pacific and Norfolk voted in favor of the $85 billion deal.

The deal, which has drawn a positive response from U.S. President Donald Trump, is still subject to regulatory clearance from the Surface Transportation Board.

The merger will create the nation's first coast-to-coast freight rail operator and reshape the movement of goods from grains to autos across the country.

data.title: LMFA: Q3 revenue up 74% YoY, mining margins at 49%, and treasury Bitcoin nearly double market cap

data.title: E-commerce services provider iPower Q1 loss narrows on cost cuts, revenue drops
data.description:
Overview

  • iPower fiscal Q1 2026 revenue fell to $12 mln from $19 mln year-over-year

  • Net loss improved to $0.5 mln from $2.1 mln in fiscal Q1 2025

  • Total debt reduced by 48% to $1.9 mln as of September 30, 2025

Outlook

  • iPower focuses on building a more efficient and profitable organization

  • iPower enhances supply chain by shifting away from China-centric model

Result Drivers

  • COST MANAGEMENT - CEO Lawrence Tan credits disciplined cost management and targeted reductions in operating expenses for improved bottom line

  • SUPPLY CHAIN SHIFT - Transition to a diversified, domestically anchored supply chain has reduced tariff-related volatility and improved logistical control

  • JOINT VENTURE BENEFITS - U.S. joint-venture manufacturing line scaling supports margin stability and operational resilience
